name: National Information Technology Development Agency
statute: Code of Practice for Interactive Computer Service Platforms / Internet Intermediaries (2022)

obligations.title: Incorporate a Nigerian legal entity (platforms of significant presence)
obligations.description: Interactive computer service platforms of significant presence must incorporate a legal entity in Nigeria and appoint a country representative accessible to regulators.

obligations.title: Designate an in-country liaison for law enforcement requests
obligations.description: Publish and maintain a designated liaison with a Nigerian address and 24/7 response capability for lawful takedown and disclosure requests.

obligations.title: Act on lawful takedown notices within 24 hours
obligations.description: Remove or disable access to non-consensual intimate imagery, CSAM, or content that is the subject of a lawful takedown order within 24 hours of notice.

obligations.title: Operate a complaint handling mechanism
obligations.description: Provide a mechanism for users to report illegal, harmful, or policy-violating content and acknowledge reports within 48 hours.

obligations.title: Submit annual compliance report to NITDA
obligations.description: File an annual compliance report covering takedowns, user complaints, moderation actions, and disclosed account information.

obligations.title: Maintain NDPR / NDPA filings with NITDA-licensed DPCO
obligations.description: Carry over NDPR-era audit filings (pre-2023 data) where still within retention windows; align new filings with NDPC.
